I've seen apps where I share a couple things that we were brainstorming to get it where I feel comfortable and good about it. So a couple things. Um, now, um, in the, the recession for golf happened a little bit later than other businesses, but I remember back in 2008, I went to chance work a lot of businesses and when the recession started hitting something really interesting happened. A lot of these companies like, Oh no, things are, things are starting to shrink, business starting to struggle and guess what they did. Most companies went and they took the marketing and the sales team. So like, Oh, these guys are the highest paid and they cut them off. And when they cut off the marketing and sales team, guess what happened to the business? It died. Do you not understand the marketing and sales is the blood of your company. Okay. If you got sick, would you cut your heart out of your body? No, that that's what's pumping the blood that keeps you alive. In 2008, all these companies thought marketing sales, this is the most expensive for the company to cut it off. As soon as they cut it off, it killed their business. It is the lifeblood. Okay. Now the same thing is happening in the golf industry. People understand this. Like, like what your course looks like is the lifeblood of your company. Okay. Um, the ones who do that, um, they want, they're there because they want the best experience. These top 100 thing that they all have in common, 100% of the top 100 common is they're the most expensive golf courses in their area. Is that weird? So what's happening is all these golf courses around them are lowering their prices, trying to like beat each other on price, lowering price, lowering price, lowering price. And there's usually one or two companies in the middle that are raising their prices and raising their prices and raising their prices. What's interesting is that the one off golfers go to the cheap ones, but the members start saying, this one looks a lot nicer. They're spending way more money maintaining things. They're giving us a better experience. When I show up, it's nicer. My clubs are new. My shoes are new. Like, they have great happy people when I show up. They hire the best talent. Okay. And the reason why is because they're charging the most, not the least. Cause the mindset you have is like my margin or tide, I need to do all these things alone at my price. It's where like you have a stock tip and you know, like, oh my gosh, this company's selling tomorrow. Let me go buy a bunch of stock or sell a bunch of stock. Right. That's illegal. Do you know why it's illegal? Because if you do that, you guarantee you're going to win every single time. Okay. The SEC will shut you down, lock you up and you're in trouble. I said, Martha Stewart, remember her? She went to jail because of insider trading. This is like insider trading except for it's not illegal.
